nodes.body: Nina and Theo are best friends. One sunny afternoon, they find something special under Grandpa's big oak tree. They pull out an old, rolled-up map. It is hand-drawn. A dotted line twists and turns on it. A red X marks the end. The two friends look at each other and smile. Today they will hunt for treasure!

choices.label: Follow the map to the great old oak.
Follow the map to the garden shed.
Follow the map down to the stream.

nodes.body: The dotted line on the map points to the great big oak. Nina holds the map while Theo counts their steps. The oak tree is huge. Three kids could hide behind it. As they walk closer, they see the dotted line split. It makes two paths near the roots of the tree.

choices.label: Search around the roots of the oak.

nodes.body: The dotted line splits into two paths now. One path wiggles toward a mossy hollow log. They hear soft croaking from it. The other path curves toward an old tire swing. It hangs from a low branch. Which path should they follow? Nina and Theo look at each other and choose their way.

choices.label: Take the trail to the hollow log.
Take the trail to the tire swing.

nodes.body: They follow the path to the hollow log. It sits in the long grass. The log is bigger than a barrel. One end is dark and cool. They hear a friendly croaking sound from deep inside. It does not sound scary at all. It sounds like tiny friends having fun.

Nina shines her flashlight into the hollow log. Theo checks the dotted line on the map. The line seems to go right through the log and out the other side. The croaking grows louder and busier. It sounds like many tiny voices all talking.

choices.label: Follow the map through the log.

nodes.body: Past the log, they see a water mark on the map. Two small paths lead away from the log now. One path is dry and dusty and goes toward the fence. The other path is soft and mossy and goes to the damp corner. Which path does the mapmaker mean? They need to decide.

The dry path just curves back to the fence. It ends at a pile of old flowerpots. There is no X here. No treasure. Nina and Theo laugh at the silly dead end. They roll up the map and skip back to the porch. They get a big cold glass of lemonade. They will try again tomorrow.

They follow the mossy path forward. It is springy and cool under their feet. Ferns brush their knees as they walk. The croaking grows louder and happier. Theo whispers that it sounds like hundreds of tiny voices talking. The friends press on deeper into the garden.

The path dips down into a shady green hollow below them. The ground here is soft under their feet. They see smooth flat stones. Each stone looks like a tiny doorway or a small home. The friends step with care and follow the dotted line deeper.

choices.label: Step into the shady green hollow.

nodes.body: In the green hollow they find a ring of round pebbles. It is set out like a tiny wall or a circle. Behind it sit plump spotted toads. The toads sit on leaf-thrones and lily-pad chairs. The toads do not hop away. They blink slowly at the visitors.

choices.label: Tiptoe up to the ring of pebbles.

nodes.body: The biggest toad wears a crown made of clover. It blinks slowly at the two friends. Nina and Theo kneel down very gently so they will not scare anyone. And there, right where the toad king sits, the map's red X is drawn.

choices.label: Kneel down by the toad king.

nodes.body: The friends have found a tiny toad kingdom in the shady hollow. A big toad with a clover crown is the king. He sits on his leafy throne. Nina and Theo promise to keep it safe. They leave a green leaf as a gift. All the way home they cannot stop smiling.

They follow the loop of the path to the old tire swing. It hangs from a low branch of the oak tree. The tire turns slowly in the breeze. Someone has tied bright ribbons to the rope. The ribbons flutter and shine in the sun.

choices.label: Look at the ribbons on the swing.

nodes.body: Theo gives the tire swing a gentle push. He hears a soft tinkling sound from higher up in the tree. Nina looks at the map. The dotted line climbs up the trunk toward the leaves. The tinkling sounds just like tiny bells.

The dotted line splits at a knot in the tree trunk. One branch of the line goes left toward thick prickly leaves. The other branch goes right along a smooth low limb. It looks easy to walk on. The friends hear the ringing bells above them. Which way should they go?

choices.label: Walk along the smooth low limb.

nodes.body: The prickly left branch is just a tangle of twigs and branches. It leads nowhere but to an empty old bird box. No X here. The friends giggle at the silly dead end. They hop off and head back. They know the X is hiding somewhere else.

The smooth low limb is easy to balance along. The tinkling gets clearer with every step they take. Nina keeps one hand on the trunk for safety. Theo watches the map and points the way. The branch curves toward a sunny gap in the leaves.
They reach a special place. Hundreds of little things dangle from twigs. Keys, shells, spoons, and buttons all knock together in the wind. The whole tree seems to sing and make music softly.

Someone made a wind chime long ago on every branch. Sunlight bounces off the shiny bits. It makes little rainbows. Nina and Theo stand still. They just listen to the music. The wind moves and makes it sing.
In the middle hangs the biggest chime. It is made from an old can and spoons. The map's red X is right beside it. Nina reaches out and gives it a very soft, gentle touch.

The friends have found a secret wind-chime tree. It sings all by itself whenever the breeze blows through. They add one small shell of their own to the branches. Now the tree will sing a little louder and sweeter. They walk home humming the tree's happy tune.

The dotted line points across the lawn to the little green shed. It sits near the fence at the back of the garden. Theo hops over a colorful flowerbed while Nina reads the map. The shed door hangs open just a crack. Something inside the shed gives a soft creak.

choices.label: Sneak up to the garden shed.

nodes.body: Behind the shed the trail splits into two paths. One line slips inside the shed's open door into the cool shadows. The other path runs off toward the big grey rock pile stacked against the back fence. The friends pick which one to explore.

choices.label: Head to the big rock pile.

nodes.body: They push the door open slowly. Dusty light comes in from outside. They see pots and hoses inside. It smells like dirt and old wood. The dotted line leads to the corner way in back.

choices.label: Follow the light to the corner.

nodes.body: Nina tiptoes carefully past rakes and watering cans. Theo holds the map steady in his hands. In the corner they see a stack of wooden crates piled up like steps going up. A faint light glows from a gap near the roof. Something up there looks cozy and warm.

choices.label: Look at the stack of crates.

nodes.body: The map is torn right at this spot in the journey. Two ways go forward from here. A low gap leads under a workbench into the dark. A wobbly ladder of crates climbs up toward the glowing gap near the roof. The friends decide which way the X wants them to go.

The low gap under the workbench is just a cobwebby dead end. It is full of old plant pots and a sleepy woodlouse. The friends back out sneezing and laughing. They dust the map off gently. They trot back to the porch for cold lemonade before trying another trail.

The crate ladder wobbles just a little but it holds strong. The friends climb toward the warm glowing gap up high. Theo goes first and gasps softly at what he sees. Nina scrambles up right behind him as fast as she can.
Up in the loft is a cozy hideaway. Someone built it from soft things. There are cushions and a table made from a crate. A light hangs down. It feels warm and nice here.
Inside are two soft seats to sit in. There are books about fun trips. A jar of shiny paper stars sits there. The map's red X is right in this special place.
The sign says Explorers Welcome. The kids know kids made this fort. Long ago they built it and left it. Now these kids can come in. It feels like they are honored guests.

The friends have found a snug secret fort hidden in the top of the shed. It is all ready and waiting for new explorers like them. They write their own names in the little logbook on the shelf. They promise to come back and take good care of it. This will be their special headquarters now.
The other path leads to the big grey rocks. They are stacked up against the back fence. The sun made them warm. Small flowers grow in between the rocks. A flat stone is near the bottom.

choices.label: Look at the low flat stone.

nodes.body: Theo taps the flat stone carefully and it sounds hollow. He can tell there is a space underneath it. Nina checks the map. The red X sits just below the rocks. A little ring of pebbles seems to mark the very spot.

Two small arrows are scratched faintly on the flat stone. One arrow points left toward a gap between two mossy boulders. The other arrow points down at the loose sandy soil right beneath the stone. The friends choose where to search and look.

The gap between the mossy boulders is just a snug home for a busy line of ants. The ants march back and forth doing their work. No treasure to be seen here. The friends say a polite hello to the ants. They roll up the map and wander back to the porch for a cold lemonade break.
They lift the stone up and move it to the side. The dirt under it is soft and easy to move. Theo digs with a tool while Nina uses her hands. Soon they see something smooth and round.

A little tin box with a rusty latch sits nestled in the sand. It is exactly where the map's X promised it would be. It is exactly the size for two hands to lift. The friends carefully wiggle it free and blow the dust off the lid.

The latch pops open with a happy click sound. Inside, wrapped in an old checkered cloth, is a collection of glass marbles. They catch the sunlight in swirls of blue, green, and gold. Nina gasps with delight at the sight.

There are cats-eyes and swirlies and one big shooter. The shooter is as clear as a raindrop. Dozens of marbles were saved by some other kid years and years ago. A tiny note in the box simply says share and enjoy. The friends read it twice and smile wide.

The friends have dug up a hidden box of beautiful glass marbles. They were left as treasure by a kid from long ago. They choose two favorite marbles to keep as memories. They leave the rest tucked back with a note of their own for the next finder. They walk home clinking a marble in each pocket.

The last part of the dotted line runs down the sloping lawn. It goes to the little stream at the very bottom of the garden. The stream is where it meets the park beyond. The water chatters happily over smooth stones. Theo holds the map up high so it will not get wet.

choices.label: Head down to the little stream.

nodes.body: Down by the water the map's line splits one final time. One branch curves into the tall blackberry bushes along the bank. The other hops out across a row of flat stepping stones. The stones cross to the far side of the stream. The friends decide which way the treasure lies.

choices.label: Follow the line into the brambles.

nodes.body: They follow the line into the shade of the tall blackberry bushes. They are heavy with ripe purple berries. The leaves make a cool green tunnel. From somewhere deeper inside comes a soft, friendly meow. It sounds calm and nice.

choices.label: Follow the meow into the bushes.

nodes.body: Nina holds a branch aside so no thorns will catch their sleeves. The meowing comes again, closer this time. Theo checks the map. The dotted line winds right into the middle of the patch. They step in gently and carefully.

choices.label: Hold the thorns aside and go in.

nodes.body: Inside the bushes the trail on the map is faded and hard to see. Two little tunnels lead on through the leaves and branches. One tunnel goes low toward a dark thorny thicket. The other goes toward a patch of warm sunlight. The meowing sounds happiest there. They pick a way.

The low tunnel just ends in a thick tangle of thorns too prickly to pass. A lost sock is caught on a branch there. The friends carefully back out without a single scratch on them. They laugh at the silly sock. They head up to the porch for a cool glass of lemonade.

The sunny tunnel opens into a cozy little clearing right in the heart of the bushes. The ground is floored with soft dry leaves. The meowing turns into a rumbly purr. Two green eyes blink at them from a warm nest of grass.

A fluffy grey cat with white paws is curled up in the sunny clearing. It does not run away at all. Instead it stretches big and yawns wide. It pads right over to say hello. It wears a little collar with a bell.

choices.label: Say hello to the fluffy cat.

nodes.body: The cat winds around their ankles, purring like a tiny motor. It floops over on its side for a belly rub. The friends laugh and stroke its soft fur gently. The map's red X is drawn right where the cat has made its cozy hideaway.

choices.label: Give the cat a belly rub.

nodes.body: The collar has a small tag that reads Pepper, please be my friend. There is no address on it at all. The cat clearly knows this bramble clearing well. It treats the friends like old pals it has known. Nina and Theo wonder if Pepper has been waiting for the map to be found.
The friends have found a cat named Pepper. He lives happy in a secret bramble spot. They share their snack with their new pal. They promise to visit him every day. Pepper walks them home.

The stepping stones cross the chattering stream in a neat flat row. They are dry on top and easy to hop from one to the next. The friends cross one careful stone at a time to the mossy far bank. The dotted line points to the roots of a leaning willow tree.

choices.label: Hop across to the far bank.

nodes.body: On the far bank the willow trails its long green fingers in the water. The ground beneath its roots is soft and cool. Theo studies the map carefully. The red X sits tucked among the tangled roots. Something glints faintly in the shade.

Two hollows show among the willow roots. One is a wet muddy dip right at the water's edge. The other is a dry sheltered nook a little higher up. The glinting seems to come from the dry spot. The friends choose where the X is pointing.
The mud spot only has a shiny wet rock and a bug there. The bug looks shocked and surprised to see them. The kids get mud on their shoes. They laugh about it. They hop back over the rocks one by one.
The dry spot is just right to kneel in. They see something shiny in the soil. Nina brushes the dirt away. Theo holds the leaves back and keeps them back. A bit of metal appears.

It is a small brass tin, green at the edges but still shut tight. It is exactly where the map's X promised. The friends work the stiff lid with careful patient fingers. At last it opens with a soft puff of old air.
Inside the tin are many shiny buttons. So many pretty buttons. Some are brass and some are glass. Some look like tiny flowers. They shine like real gold in the light.
Someone kept these buttons safe here for many years. Each button is pretty and special and unique. A note inside says for the finder, with love. The kids feel like they found real gold.

The friends have dug up a hidden tin of shiny buttons. It is the real gift at the end of the map. They pick one button each to keep as their own. They put it back with a note for the next finder. They walk home across the rocks feeling like kings.
